items.title: Treasuries Set for Best Week Since War Began as Oil Retreats
items.summary: The US government bond market headed for its best week since the start of the US war on Iran as oil prices declined in anticipation of an agreement to end the conflict.

Fed’s Bowman Says Too Soon to Judge Iran War Inflation Impact
items.summary: Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman said, “I would like to have more clarity on the economic impacts from the conflict in the Middle East and the durability of those effects,” and thinks it’s too soon to judge the inflationary impact from the Iran war. items.title: Quarter of Big Oil Tankers Trapped by Iran War Have Escaped
items.summary: Roughly one-quarter of the non-Iranian large oil tankers trapped inside the Persian Gulf at the outbreak of the Iran war have managed to slip out in a slow, stealthy trickle.

items.title:
I thank Japan for its contribution to the PURL initiative. Since the very beginning of Russia’s full-scale invasion, Japan has been helping Ukraine and our people a lot. And we deeply appreciate all the support we have received.

I also thank all partners who continue to contribute to PURL. This year, the program has been supported by Norway, the Netherlands, Canada, Bulgaria, Belgium, the United Kingdom, Spain, Sweden, Lithuania, Estonia, Latvia, and Iceland. Some of the contributions were not publicly disclosed. The total amount of contributions since the beginning of the year has reached around $1.5 billion. And altogether, 28 countries have participated in PURL up until this point. I thank all partners who are helping!
